sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board didn't reset after clicking on the purple cursor. The green cells are still at (19,42), (25,42), (37,42). So the purple cursor doesn't reset the board anymore, or maybe it's not the submit button.
Let me check if there's a purple cursor on the board. Looking at the segmentation, I don't see any purple nodes. So maybe the purple cursor was removed or it's at a different position.
Let me check the segmentation for all node colors.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check all node colors
seg = current_frame.segmentation
colors = set()
for n in seg['nodes']:
    colors.add(n['color'])
print(f"Colors: {colors}")

# Check for purple nodes
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"p: center=({cr},{cc}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check ascii for purple 'p' characters
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(64):
    row = ascii_lines[r]
    special = [(i, c) for i, c in enumerate(row) if c == 'p']
    if special:
        print(f"Row {r} p: {special}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple cursor is gone. The colors are: S (sky blue), w (light gray), b (blue), B (black), N (light green/green), W (white).
So the purple cursor disappeared after clicking on it. The board state is:
- 3 green cells at (19,42), (25,42), (37,42)
- No purple cursor
The level is still not complete. Maybe the goal is not to have 3 green cells at column 42. Let me think about what the goal might be:
1. Maybe the goal is to have all green cells in a single column (which we have)
2. Maybe the goal is to have a specific number of green cells
3. Maybe the goal is to arrange green cells in a specific pattern
Let me try a different approach. Maybe I need to merge the green cells further. Let me select one of the green cells and see where the g overlay appears.
Let me select (19,42) which is the top green cell in column 42.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Select (19,42) and check for g overlay
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 42}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"{n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

N: center=(19,42)
N: center=(25,42)
g: center=(30,42)
g: center=(31,41)
g: center=(31,44)
g: center=(33,42)
N: center=(37,42)
